info.title: Roam HQ Events API (Alpha)
info.description:
The Roam HQ Events API (Alpha) delivers real-time notifications to your application via webhooks. Subscribe to events and receive HTTP callbacks when things happen in your Roam workspace.

**OpenAPI Spec:** [webhooks.json](https://developer.ro.am/webhooks.json)

## Configuring Webhooks

You can configure webhooks in two ways:
- **Static:** In **Roam Administration > Developer > API Client**, add webhook URLs directly to your app configuration
- **Dynamic:** Use the subscription endpoints below to manage webhooks programmatically

## Subscription Endpoints

| Endpoint | Method | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| [`/webhook.subscribe`](/docs/webhooks/webhook-subscribe) | POST | Create or update a webhook subscription |
| [`/webhook.unsubscribe`](/docs/webhooks/webhook-unsubscribe) | POST | Remove a webhook subscription |

## Available Events

| Event | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| [`chat:message:dm`](/docs/webhooks/chat-message) | Direct message received by your app |
| [`chat:message:channel`](/docs/webhooks/chat-message) | Channel message in a group where your app is a member |
| [`chat:message:mention`](/docs/webhooks/chat-message) | Message that @mentions your app |
| [`chat:message:reaction`](/docs/webhooks/chat-message-reaction) | Emoji reaction added to a message |
| [`recording:saved`](/docs/webhooks/recording-saved) | Meeting recording is ready for download |
| [`transcript:started`](/docs/webhooks/transcript-started) | Magic Minutes has started on a meeting |
| [`transcript:saved`](/docs/webhooks/transcript-saved) | Meeting transcript (Magic Minutes) is available |
| [`lobby:booked`](/docs/webhooks/lobby-booked) | New booking created for a lobby |
| [`user:status:update`](/docs/webhooks/user-status-update) | User checked in or out of the Roam |
| [`onair.event.created`](/docs/webhooks/onair-event-created) | On-Air event is created |
| [`onair.event.updated`](/docs/webhooks/onair-event-updated) | On-Air event is updated |
| [`onair.event.canceled`](/docs/webhooks/onair-event-canceled) | On-Air event is canceled |
| [`onair.guest.rsvp`](/docs/webhooks/onair-guest-rsvp) | Guest RSVP status changed |
| [`onair.guest.added`](/docs/webhooks/onair-guest-added) | Guest(s) added to an event |
| [`magicast.created`](/docs/webhooks/magicast-created) | A magicast is created and ready |

## Access Models

Webhooks support both [Organization access and Personal access](/docs/guides/access-models). The same events are available in both modes, but the scope differs:

- **Organization access:** Webhooks deliver events for all activity across the workspace (e.g. all messages in public groups, all meeting transcripts).
- **Personal access:** Webhooks deliver only events involving the authenticated user (e.g. only your DMs, only your meeting transcripts).

See the [Access Models guide](/docs/guides/access-models) for details on choosing the right model.

## Webhook Payload Format

All webhooks are delivered as POST requests with JSON payloads. Your endpoint should return a 2xx status to acknowledge receipt.

## Signature Verification

Webhooks are signed using the [Standard Webhooks](https://github.com/standard-webhooks/standard-webhooks) specification. Each request includes three headers for verification:

| Header | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `webhook-id` | Unique identifier for this webhook delivery |
| `webhook-timestamp` | Unix timestamp (seconds) when the webhook was sent |
| `webhook-signature` | HMAC-SHA256 signature of the payload |

Your **Webhook Signing Secret** is available in **Roam Administration > Developer > API Client**.

To verify a webhook:
1. Concatenate: `{webhook-id}.{webhook-timestamp}.{payload}`
2. Compute HMAC-SHA256 using your signing secret (base64-decoded)
3. Compare with the signature in `webhook-signature` header

We recommend using the [standard-webhooks client libraries](https://github.com/standard-webhooks/standard-webhooks#libraries) for verification:

```javascript
import { Webhook } from "standardwebhooks";

const wh = new Webhook(signingSecret);
const payload = wh.verify(requestBody, requestHeaders);
```

## Delivery Behavior

- **Timeout**: Webhook requests timeout after **3 seconds**. Ensure your endpoint responds quickly.
- **Retries**: Webhooks are **not automatically retried**. If your endpoint returns a non-2xx status or times out, the delivery is logged but not reattempted.
- **Order**: Webhooks are delivered asynchronously and may arrive out of order.

For reliable processing, we recommend:
- Acknowledge webhooks immediately with a 200 response
- Process webhook data asynchronously in a background job
- Use the `webhook-id` header for idempotency

## Filtering

Some events support filters to limit notifications:
- `lobby:booked`: Filter by `lobbyId` to receive bookings for specific lobbies only
- `chat:message:reaction`: Filter by `codes` to receive only specific reaction types
- `onair.event.created`, `onair.event.updated`, `onair.event.canceled`, `onair.guest.added`: Filter by `eventId` to receive notifications for a specific event only
- `onair.guest.rsvp`: Filter by `eventId` and/or `status` to receive notifications for a specific event or RSVP status

## Authentication

```
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN
```

## Base URL

```
https://api.ro.am/v0
```

---
Have questions? Contact us via [Roam Support Chat](https://ro.am/support/contact-us) or email [developer@ro.am](mailto:developer@ro.am).

post.description:
Create or update a webhook subscription for a given event. If a subscription
already exists for the same event and URL, its filter is updated instead of
creating a duplicate.

**Access:** Organization only.

**Required scope:** `webhook:write`

---

**OpenAPI Spec:** [webhooks.json](https://developer.ro.am/webhooks.json)

onair-guest-rsvp.summary: Subscribe to On-Air guest RSVP changes

post.description:
Remove a webhook subscription by ID.

**Access:** Organization only.

**Required scope:** `webhook:write`

---

**OpenAPI Spec:** [webhooks.json](https://developer.ro.am/webhooks.json)

A chat message addressed to the app.

Webhooks are delivered only when:
- Someone sends a direct message (DM) to the app
- The app is added to a chat group (channel) and that group receives messages
- Someone @mentions the app in a message

The Events API does NOT deliver every chat message in the account—only those where the app is a participant or mentioned.

**Available events:**
- `chat:message:dm` — Direct messages to the app
- `chat:message:channel` — Messages in groups where the app is a member
- `chat:message:mention` — Messages that @mention the app (useful for bots that only respond when called)

200.description: Return a 200 status to indicate that the data was received successfully

post.description:
A chat message addressed to the app.

Webhooks are delivered only when:
- Someone sends a direct message (DM) to the app
- The app is added to a chat group (channel) and that group receives messages
- Someone @mentions the app in a message

The Events API does NOT deliver every chat message in the account—only those where the app is a participant or mentioned.

**Available events:**
- `chat:message:dm` — Direct messages to the app
- `chat:message:channel` — Messages in groups where the app is a member
- `chat:message:mention` — Messages that @mention the app (useful for bots that only respond when called)

200.description: Return a 200 status to indicate that the data was received successfully

post.description:
A reaction was added to a chat message.

**Event name:** `chat:message:reaction`

Webhooks are delivered when someone adds a reaction to a message in a chat where the app is a participant.

200.description: Return a 200 status to indicate that the data was received successfully

post.description:
A meeting recording (video) has been finalized and is now available for download / playback.

**Event name:** `recording:saved`

**Required scopes:** Your app must have `user:read` and `user:read.email` scopes to receive participant information in the webhook payload. Without these scopes, participant details will be omitted.

200.description: Return a 200 status to acknowledge receipt of the event

post.description:
Magic Minutes has started on a meeting and a live transcript is now available.

**Event name:** `transcript:started`

Use the transcript `id` from this payload with
[`/transcript.info`](/docs/chat-api/get-transcript) to fetch live transcript
content. Pass the `sinceOffset` parameter to poll for incremental updates.

**Required scope:** `transcript:read`

The `hostEmail` field is only included if your app has the `user:read.email` scope.

id.description: The transcript ID. Use with `/transcript.info` to fetch transcript content.

hostEmail.description: Email of the meeting host. Only included if your app has the `user:read.email` scope.

200.description: Return a 200 status to acknowledge receipt of the event

post.description:
A meeting transcript (Magic Minutes) has been finalized and is now available.

**Event name:** `transcript:saved`

This webhook provides transcript metadata only. To retrieve the full transcript
content including cues (speaker text), summary, and action items, use
[`/transcript.info`](/docs/chat-api/get-transcript) with the transcript `id` from this payload.

**Required scopes:** Your app must have `user:read` and `user:read.email` scopes to receive participant information in the webhook payload. Without these scopes, participant details will be omitted.

200.description: Return a 200 status to acknowledge receipt of the event

post.description:
A new booking has been created for a lobby.

**Event name:** `lobby:booked`

This event fires when a guest completes booking for a lobby (via a lobby link/handle).

200.description: Return a 200 status to acknowledge receipt of the event

post.description:
A user's presence status has changed (checked in or checked out of the Roam).

**Event name:** `user:status:update`

The webhook payload contains the full user object with the updated `status` field.

**Required scopes:** Your app must have `user:read` scope to receive this event. Add `user:read.email` to include the user's email address in the payload.

200.description: Return a 200 status to acknowledge receipt of the event

post.description:
A new On-Air event has been created.

**Event name:** `onair.event.created`

**Required scope:** `onair:read`

200.description: Return a 200 status to acknowledge receipt of the event

post.description:
An On-Air event has been updated (e.g. title, time, or settings changed).

**Event name:** `onair.event.updated`

**Required scope:** `onair:read`

200.description: Return a 200 status to acknowledge receipt of the event

post.description:
An On-Air event has been canceled.

**Event name:** `onair.event.canceled`

**Required scope:** `onair:read`

200.description: Return a 200 status to acknowledge receipt of the event

post.description:
A guest's RSVP status has changed for an On-Air event.

**Event name:** `onair.guest.rsvp`

**Required scope:** `onair:read`

200.description: Return a 200 status to acknowledge receipt of the event

post.description:
One or more guests have been added to an On-Air event.

**Event name:** `onair.guest.added`

**Required scope:** `onair:read`

200.description: Return a 200 status to acknowledge receipt of the event

post.description:
A magicast has been created and is ready.

**Event name:** `magicast.created`

**Required scope:** `magicast:read`

200.description: Return a 200 status to acknowledge receipt of the event

WebhookSubscriptionFilter.description: Event-specific filter to limit webhook notifications. Different properties apply to different events.

lobbyId.description: For `lobby.booked`: restrict to bookings for the specified lobby.

codes.description: For `chat.message.reaction`: restrict to the specified reaction codes (e.g. 'thumbs_up', 'heart').

eventId.description: For On-Air events (`onair.event.*`, `onair.guest.*`): restrict to the specified event.

status.description: For `onair.guest.rsvp`: restrict to the specified RSVP status.

url.description: Destination URL for webhook deliveries. HTTPS is required outside local environments.

id.description: Unique identifier of the webhook subscription.

filter.description: Event-specific filter applied to the subscription.

id.description: Identifier of the webhook subscription to remove.

TaggedUUID.description: A UUID prefixed by a tag identifying the specific type of object

type.description:
Type of item:

- **photo**: Images with inline preview and thumbnail
  - image/jpeg, image/png, image/gif, image/webp

- **blob**: Any other file type (download only, no preview)
  - application/octet-stream

mime.description:
MIME type of the file (e.g., "application/octet-stream").
May be omitted for photo items where the type is inferred from the image format.

created.description: Timestamp when the item was created

name.description: Name of the item (typically the filename).

thumbnail.description:
URL for a thumbnail of the uploaded item (photo type only).
This may be equal to the item's main URL if it is suitable to use as a thumbnail.

size.description: Size of the item in bytes

imageUrl.description: URL of the user's profile image

email.description: Email address of the user (requires `user:read.email` scope)

isAdmin.description: Whether the user is an admin of the Roam

status.description: User's current presence status. Only included when `expand=status` is requested and the `user:read.status` scope is granted.

Reaction.description: A reaction added to a chat message

chat.description: The chat containing the message that was reacted to.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of the message that was reacted to.

name.description: Name of the reaction that was added (e.g. "thumbs_up", "heart").

user.description: The user who added the reaction.

chat.description: ID of the chat in which the message was sent

threadTimestamp.description: Key of the message that this is a reply to. Omitted if this is not a reply.

timestamp.description: Key of the message within a chat, as Unix microseconds

text.description: Text of the message, formatted as github-flavored markdown

blocks.description: Block Kit blocks, present when contentType is `block`. See the [Block Kit guide](/docs/guides/block-kit).

color.description: Color strip on the message. One of: `good`, `warning`, `danger`, or a hex color like `#5B3FD9`. Present only on Block Kit messages.

poll.description: Poll content, present when contentType is `poll`.

allowMultipleAnswers.description: Whether voters can select multiple options.

closesAt.description: When the poll closes (RFC-3339). Omitted if no close time is set.

replyTimestamp.description: Key of the message that this is a reply to. This applies only in DM chats which lack real threads.

email.description: Primary email address of the participant (if available)

location.description: Geographic region where the recording took place / storage region

startTime.description: ISO-8601 start time of the recording

endTime.description: ISO-8601 end time of the recording

videoUrl.description: Direct download URL for the video (if enabled)

transcriptId.description: Identifier for the associated transcript (if available)

unconfirmedEmail.description: Self-reported email address provided by a guest participant. Not verified — the guest was not logged in.

type.description: Whether the participant is a workspace member or a guest

text.description: The transcribed text of what was said.

startOffset.description: Milliseconds from the start of the transcript when the utterance began.

endOffset.description: Milliseconds from the start of the transcript when the utterance ended.

title.description: Short name for the action item.

description.description: Additional context or details about the action item.

assignee.description: Person responsible for the action item, if known.

id.description: A unique identifier for the transcript

meetingId.description:
A unique identifier for the meeting.
A meeting may encompass 0 or multiple transcripts.

start.description: Exact time when the transcript began

end.description: Exact time when the transcript stopped

eventName.description: Name of meeting event associated with the transcript (optional)

actionItems.description: Action items identified during the meeting.

meetingLinkId.description: Meeting link identifier, if this transcript is from a scheduled calendar event

invitees.description: Email addresses of calendar event invitees

id.description: Unique identifier for the lobby configuration

active.description: Whether the lobby is currently active

handle.description: The handle extracted from the lobby URL, if available

isOrganizer.description: Whether this host is the organizer

isBooker.description: Whether this invitee created the booking

timeZone.description: IANA time zone of the booking times

notes.description: Optional notes provided by the booker

meetingLink.description: Meeting link URL, used to join the meeting

imageUrl.description: URL of the host's profile image.

eventPageUrl.description: Public URL of the event page.

enableSEO.description: Whether the event page is indexed by search engines.

autoAdmit.description: Whether guests are automatically admitted when they join.

disableRSVP.description: Whether RSVPs are disabled for this event.

hosts.description: List of hosts for the event.

eventId.description: Identifier of the event this guest belongs to.

updated.description: When the guest record was last updated (RFC 3339).

createdAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the magicast was created (UTC)

ownerId.description: Address ID of the magicast owner

coverImageUrl.description: URL for the magicast cover image thumbnail
